notes: FTS5 virtual table existence probe; Turso lacks FTS5 — this check and all memory_fts consumers must be replaced or gated
ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN DDL; supported by Turso but PRAGMA table_info introspection path differs in libSQL
PRAGMA table_info used for migration idempotency check; libSQL supports PRAGMA but returns different metadata shape in some versions
ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN requires SQLite 3.35.0+; Turso/libSQL supports but verify version compatibility
FTS5 gate: determines whether SQLite FTS5 or in-memory BM25 is used; Turso migration must replace this with alternative lexical engine detection
Engine routing enum: 'sqlite' mode requires FTS5; Turso migration must add a new engine value or repurpose 'sqlite' to mean libSQL-compatible lexical
Decision function for FTS5 vs in-memory BM25; Turso migration must redirect this to non-FTS5 lexical path
Safety check that learned_triggers stays out of FTS5 index; concept transfers to Turso but implementation must target replacement index
Channel routing config includes 'fts' and 'bm25' channels; Turso migration must remap 'fts' channel to non-FTS5 lexical backend
Quality-gap fallback explicitly names 'fts5' tier; Turso migration must rename or replace this tier identifier
Fallback mode string references fts5; Turso migration must update enum value or add alternative mode
Guards all sqlite-fts lexical paths; Turso has no FTS5 virtual tables, so memory_fts will never exist — every FTS5-dependent branch dead-ends.
auto mode falls back to JS BM25 when FTS5 absent; on Turso this path always fires — in-memory index becomes sole lexical engine.
Reads FTS5 CREATE statement from sqlite_master; Turso has no memory_fts table so verification trivially passes — but callers expecting a definitive check may misbehave.
Engine selection must default away from sqlite on Turso; auto mode needs Turso-aware detection or will try sqlite path and fail.
Orchestrates channel selection; on Turso the 'fts' channel is dead (no FTS5), so every routing tier loses one channel — minimum-channel enforcement may pad unexpectedly.
better-sqlite3 Statement type assertion; Turso returns different statement interface, all casts must be reviewed
FTS5 virtual table existence check; Turso lacks FTS5, this gate will always return false, disabling sqlite BM25 engine path
Reads FTS5 CREATE statement to verify isolation; Turso has no FTS5 so memory_fts won't exist, isolation check becomes vacuous
Transitive better-sqlite3 dependency via vector-index module; migration requires vector-index to return Turso client
Entire sqlite lexical engine path gated on FTS5 table existence; Turso migration forces all users to in-memory BM25 fallback
